content:
type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py
your_files: dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Confirmed: I own cache.py (will add both my TTL logic AND your compression spec verbatim as you described), you own __init__.py. Here is my exact TTL spec for __init__.py:

1) configure_cache(): add new param  ttl: Optional[int] = None  (docstring: 'ttl: Default time-to-live in seconds for cache entries. None means entries never expire.'). Pass ttl=ttl into the Cache(...) constructor call at the bottom of configure_cache (alongside your compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy kwargs).

2) Add a new module-level function (place near enable_litellm_logging/disable_litellm_logging):
def set_ttl(seconds: Optional[int] = None) -> None:
    import dspy
    dspy.cache.set_ttl(seconds)
(I'm adding a Cache.set_ttl(self, ttl) method in cache.py that swaps memory_cache between LRUCache/TTLCache and updates self.ttl for the disk layer, so this just delegates.)

3) Update the DSPY_CACHE = Cache(...) instantiation to add  ttl=None,  as a new kwarg (alongside your compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy kwargs).

4) Add 'set_ttl' to the __all__ list.

No other __init__.py changes needed from me. Optional is already imported there so no new imports needed for my part. Go ahead and implement - I'm starting on cache.py now with both our specs.
